select_when: The system can learn, adapt, or improve through bounded stress rather than merely survive it.
Downside can be contained while variation, feedback, and small failures create useful information or stronger designs.
Uncertainty is unavoidable and the smarter move is to gain from disorder instead of trying to predict it away.
The team needs experiments, de-risking, and feedback loops rather than brittle plans optimized for normal conditions only.
avoid_when: Volatility is being romanticized and the system is being exposed to ruinous shocks that are too large to survive or learn from.
Experts are leaning on familiar but brittle frameworks instead of investigating the novel structure of the situation.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on bounded stress, learning through variation, and anti-brittle design rather than on generic risk appetite rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style investing overlays because the raw source is stronger on iterative testing, anti-goal logic, and de-risking unknowns.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stays inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The typed fields compress a source that spans strategic experimentation, risk management, and systems adaptation.

select_when.note: The raw strengths section is explicit on bounded stress, contained downside, useful variation, and gaining from uncertainty.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by the source warnings on ruinous shocks and expert overconfidence or false pattern recognition.

input_type.note: The source activates on systems exposed to disorder and stress, but the compact input label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source yields stronger designs that benefit from disorder; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Antifragility clearly uses systems and branch-aware stress reasoning, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
